Maths - Number and operations.skill: Place value models up to thousands
Maths - Number and operations.prompt: This topic covers Place value models up to thousands for Grade 3 students (TEKS 3.2(A)). Students use visual and physical models to understand place value. Base-ten blocks, bundles of sticks, and place value charts help students see that digits in different positions represent different values. Key concepts include representing numbers with models, reading models to determine the number shown, and understanding the relationship between ones, tens, and hundreds. Vocabulary: place value, model, base-ten blocks, ones, tens, hundreds, represent, value, digit. Common misconceptions include miscounting blocks, confusing tens rods with ones cubes, and not connecting models to written numbers. Activities should include building numbers with blocks, matching models to numerals, and comparing numbers using models appropriate for ages 8-9.

Maths - Number and operations.skill: Convert between standard and expanded form
Maths - Number and operations.prompt: This topic covers Convert between standard and expanded form for Grade 3 students (TEKS 3.2(A)). Students extend place value understanding to include hundreds, writing numbers in expanded form showing the value of each digit. For example, 115 = 100 + 10 + 5. Key concepts include identifying the value of each digit based on its position, converting between standard form and expanded form, and understanding that each place value position is ten times the one to its right. Common misconceptions include confusing face value with place value and difficulty with numbers containing zero. Activities should include base-ten block building, writing equations, and number form conversion exercises appropriate for ages 8-9.
